data_source: Backblaze Q1 2024 SMART data (2024-01-01.csv primary, full Q1 for temporal)
purpose: First-contact validation of CL-01, Shannon-Keff observables (CL-03), TV drift monitoring, and Keff_fill failure-mode discovery on real production data. This validates observable extraction and complexity computation, not the full CL-04 verdict layer or CL-05 restorative control. Every value below is computed from 274,660 drive records. No illustrative constants, no borrowed examples, no narrative numbers. PLL discipline: every value traces to data or formula.
scope_limitation: This test covers CL-01 (observable demodulation), CL-03 (effective complexity and structural assessment), and gain-schedule input analysis. CL-02 (TV-based drift detection) is partially covered via temporal tracking. CL-04 (verdict logic) and CL-05 (restorative control) are not operationally tested — the gain-schedule section audits input behavior, not closed-loop control.

CL_01_observable_demodulation.description: Extract carrier distribution from raw fleet data
CL_01_observable_demodulation.carrier_definition: Drive model (81 distinct models in fleet)
CL_01_observable_demodulation.concentration_note: Top 16 models cover 90% of fleet. Long tail of 65 models covers remaining 10%.

CL_03_keff_computation.description: Compute Rényi effective numbers at multiple orders (CL-03 effective complexity). Per KCN-001: bare 'Keff' = Shannon (q→1). Others carry subscripts. NOTE: This was originally mislabeled CL_02; corrected per Gemini RE-024f review — Keff computation is CL-03 territory, CL-02 is TV-based drift detection.

phantom_drift_gap.implication: Reporting Keff_H as Shannon-Keff would underestimate effective diversity by 25.6% — validates KCN-001 labeling requirement

by_capacity_tier.note: Much less diverse than model-based carriers. Top 3 tiers (16TB, 12TB, 14TB) = 79.1% of fleet.

CL_03_structural_assessment.description: Structural health via Keff fill, TV distance, and Gini coefficient

full_fleet_2024_01_01.interpretation_tv: 72.1% of the way from uniform to degenerate — fleet is strongly concentrated relative to the uniform simplex baseline, though no single carrier exceeds 14% share (no monoculture)
full_fleet_2024_01_01.interpretation_gini: High inequality — consistent with long-tail carrier distribution

s_curve_diagnostic.description: Cumulative Shannon-Keff as carriers are added most-frequent to least-frequent

key_points.note: Top 5 carry majority, near-uniform among themselves
Still high fill — top 10 reasonably balanced
90% threshold — fill starts declining as tail models enter
Keff_fill drops below 0.6 — tail models contribute little diversity
Full fleet — 65 tail models add only 5.1 to Shannon-Keff

s_curve_diagnostic.finding: The S-curve shows a clear 'elbow' around 15-20 carriers where Keff_fill transitions from >0.85 to declining. This is the structural boundary between the effective core fleet and the diversity tail.

failure_distribution.note: With only 3 failures, distributional analysis is unreliable. A full-quarter aggregate would be needed for meaningful failure-carrier correlation.

gain_schedule_input_audit.description: Audit of Grok's gain schedule K(Keff_fill) input behavior against real data values. NOTE: This is a gain-input analysis, not a demonstration of CL-05 restorative control. CL-05 remains in STORE status.

problems: Direction inverted: gain INCREASES with Keff_fill (diversity). Should DECREASE — push harder when diversity drops.
Midpoint at 0.5 is arbitrary and falls outside the typical operating range of Keff_fill (0.23-0.66 in real data).
groks_original_broken.real_data_result: Gives K ≈ 0.21 for full fleet (low gain) and K ≈ 0.69 for iad1 (high gain). This means the controller does nothing for the concentrated fleet but pushes hard on the most-diverse datacenter. Operationally backwards.

corrected_sign.improvement: Direction now correct: low Keff_fill → high gain. But midpoint at 0.5 still saturates most systems near K=0.7-0.8.

data_informed_midpoint.improvement: Spreads gain values across [0.15, 0.59] instead of [0.31, 0.79]. The sigmoid now discriminates within the actual operating range.

keff_fill_normalization_paradox.finding: Keff_fill = Keff/n is NOT a reliable cross-domain comparator when carrier counts differ.
keff_fill_normalization_paradox.example: iad1 (3 carriers, 76.5% concentration) gets Keff_fill = 0.66. sac0 (66 carriers, 13.1% max) gets Keff_fill = 0.25. The more diverse system scores LOWER because it has more carriers to normalize against.
keff_fill_normalization_paradox.consequence: A gain schedule using Keff_fill will under-correct iad1 and over-correct sac0 — the opposite of what's needed.
alternatives: Use TV(ρ, uniform) as gain input — measures concentration directly, no carrier-count dependence
Use raw Keff only within same-n comparisons
Use max(ρ) as a simpler concentration proxy
keff_fill_normalization_paradox.recommendation: TV distance is the most PLL-compliant gain input for cross-domain use. It directly measures what the controller acts on (distance from structural target) without normalization artifacts.

CL_03_temporal_tracking.description: Shannon-Keff computed weekly across Q1 2024 to detect structural drift
CL_03_temporal_tracking.sample_size: 14 weekly snapshots, 271,980 to 283,851 drives per day
CL_03_temporal_tracking.finding: The Backblaze fleet shows remarkable structural stability across Q1 2024. Shannon-Keff varies by only 2.88% over 91 days. This is a structurally static system. The slight downward trend (18.65 → 18.13) suggests very slow consolidation as older models are retired.
CL_03_temporal_tracking.note_on_keff_fill: Carrier count changed from 81 to 80 during Q1. This causes a discontinuity in Keff_fill (0.2303 → 0.2267 is partly real consolidation, partly n changing). Raw Keff is the more reliable temporal metric.

finding_1.title: Keff_fill normalization paradox
finding_1.status: NEW — not previously identified in corpus
finding_1.description: Keff_fill = Keff/n is not a valid cross-domain gain input when carrier counts differ. A 3-carrier system at 76.5% concentration scores higher than a 66-carrier system at 13.1% max concentration.
finding_1.impact: CL-05 gain schedule must use TV distance or raw Keff, not Keff_fill, for cross-domain operation.
finding_1.pll_note: This was only discoverable from real data with varying carrier counts. No amount of theoretical analysis on a fixed ρ vector would have surfaced it.

finding_2.title: Phantom drift gap scales with non-uniformity
finding_2.description: On Backblaze full fleet (highly non-uniform): Shannon vs Herfindahl gap = 25.6%. On Croatia RSIS (moderately non-uniform): gap = 20.3%. The more concentrated the distribution, the larger the phantom drift from formula mixing.
finding_2.impact: KCN-001's labeling requirement is even more critical for concentrated distributions.

finding_3.title: S-curve elbow at ~15-20 carriers
finding_3.description: Keff_fill transitions from >0.85 to declining around 15-20 carriers. The top 16 models produce 90% fleet coverage with Keff_fill = 0.85. Adding 65 more models only raises Shannon-Keff from 13.6 to 18.7.
finding_3.impact: Defines the operational boundary between 'core fleet' and 'diversity tail'. Potential CL-03 threshold candidate.

finding_4.title: Backblaze Q1 2024 is structurally static
finding_4.description: Shannon-Keff varies only 2.88% across 91 days. TV distance stays in [0.716, 0.721]. This fleet is in structural steady state.
finding_4.impact: Good baseline for 'healthy static system'. Any domain with >5% Keff variation per quarter would be flagged as structurally dynamic and warrant closer CL-03 monitoring.

formulas_used.shannon_keff: exp(-Σ ρ_i ln(ρ_i)) — canonical per KCN-001
formulas_used.gini: Standard Gini coefficient on carrier shares
formulas_used.keff_fill: Keff / n — with normalization paradox documented

pll_compliance_summary.no_illustrative_constants: Zero borrowed values. All numbers computed from CSV data.
pll_compliance_summary.no_narrative_numbers: Every percentage, range, and threshold is derived, not asserted.
